title: Array Statistics Reporter
prompt_md:
## Goal
Declare a C-style array, fill it from standard input, and compute basic statistics.

## Input
- Line 1: an integer `n` (1 to 10) representing how many values follow.
- Line 2: `n` space-separated integers.

## Output
- Line 1: the sum of all `n` values.
- Line 2: the minimum value.
- Line 3: the maximum value.

## Requirements
1. Declare a C-style array of `int` with a fixed capacity of 10 elements.
2. Read `n`, then read exactly `n` integers into the array using a `for` loop.
3. Compute the sum, minimum, and maximum by iterating over the first `n` elements.
4. Print each result on its own line.
5. Do not use `std::array`, `std::vector`, or any container from `<algorithm>`.
6. You may use `#include <iostream>` and `#include <limits>`.

## Notes
- Values can be negative. Initialize your min/max tracking variables carefully.

title: Reverse and Count
prompt_md:
## Goal
Read values into a C-style array, print them in reverse order, and report the array's byte size.

## Input
- Line 1: an integer `n` (1 to 8) representing how many values follow.
- Line 2: `n` space-separated integers.

## Output
- Line 1: the `n` values printed in reverse order, separated by spaces (no trailing space).
- Line 2: the total byte size of the entire fixed array as reported by `sizeof`.
- Line 3: the number of elements the fixed array can hold (computed using `sizeof`).

## Requirements
1. Declare a C-style array `int arr[8]{}` with a fixed capacity of 8.
2. Read `n`, then read `n` values into the array.
3. Print the first `n` elements in reverse order on one line, separated by single spaces with no trailing space.
4. Print `sizeof(arr)` on the next line.
5. Print `sizeof(arr) / sizeof(arr[0])` on the next line.
6. Do not use `std::array`, `std::vector`, or `<algorithm>`.
7. You may use `#include <iostream>`.

## Notes
- The byte size depends on `sizeof(int)` on the target platform. The Judge0 environment uses 4-byte `int`, so `sizeof(arr)` will be 32 and the element count will be 8.
